observations.correspondence_rationale: The phrase 'Your temple fell because of a blinded man' and 'You crumpled before the sacred chest' explicitly alludes to the biblical account of the Ark of the Covenant causing the fall of the temple or sacred place, as in 1 Kings 8:10-11 where the glory of the Lord filled the temple, indicating the sacred chest (Ark) as a divine presence. The mention of the temple falling and the sacred chest evokes the biblical motif of the Ark's power and the temple's sanctity.
observations.summary: The text explicitly alludes to the biblical motif of the temple and the sacred chest (Ark of the Covenant) and its divine presence causing the temple's fall due to a blinded man, paralleling the biblical narrative of the Ark's power in the temple.

evidence.quote: "Your temple fell because of a blinded man," he snarled, the hesitation hardening. "You crumpled before the sacred chest. Why should I serve you rather than El, who dwells higher?"
